These tests involve verifying that the solution description details how encryption keys are managed, verifying that switching on and off and status of switches is not possible by integration, and verifying that it is possible to copy static fields in HES. Additionally, test case T-14 involves measuring performance during normal reporting operation and increasing CPU and RAM in clusters, which may indirectly impact the customer interface. The following test cases cover the requirement "Scaling of environment":

- T-3: Test steps: Verify that switching on and off and status of breakers from other GE system is not possible by integration. Verify that status of switches is possible to see by integration.
- T-13: Test steps: Verify that where there is a need to select dates in HES, this is done through a time and calendar function.
- T-15: Test steps: Verify that it is possible to copy static fields in HES such as headers, status, responses to Events in the system.
- T-18: Test steps: Verify that the metering device has the capability to detect connections that may cause overheating.
- T-20: Test steps: Verify that the Metering System is able to record and store information when the following Events occur: in case of connection overheating.

These test cases cover the requirement "Scaling of environment" by testing the ability of the system to handle increased data volumes and maintain required performance.

The following test IDs cover the requirement: T-7, T-11, T-12, T-13, T-15, T-16, T-17, T-18, T-19, T-20.

These tests cover the requirement for the Metering Unit to be able to detect connections that may cause overheating. The tests include checking for connection overheating in all phases (Cat 1, 2, and 3), generating high current and power events, and using a super magnet to cause an event in the meter. Additionally, the tests cover the requirement for the Metering System to be able to record and store information when certain events occur, such as connection overheating. The tests also cover the legal requirements for metering, calculation and reporting of transmitted electricity, as well as the EU regulations on data protection and the free movement of such data. The tests also cover the requirement for the Metering System to be able to be registered with the relevant authorities and to have a serial number placed on a non-removable part. The tests also cover the requirement for the Metering Units to be designed for wall mounting and to have sealing devices that are raised and clearly visible for smooth and accessible passage of the sealing wire. The tests also cover the requirement for the Metering System to have a life expectancy of 15 years from the date of delivery to GE. The tests also cover the requirement for the Metering System to be able to be used with different types of computers, tablets, and smartphones, with different architectures and operating systems. The tests also cover the requirement for the Metering System to conform to various standards and regulations, including the EU MID Directive 2014/32/EU, the Swedish Energy Markets Inspectorate's Regulations and General Advice on Metering, Calculation and Reporting of Transmitted Electricity, the Swedish Regulations on Measuring Instruments, the Extended type approval and certification of Category 1 and 2 meters according to method SPM 1618, test with 12kV surge voltage, and the EU Regulation 2016/679 on the protection of natural persons with regard to the processing of personal data and on the free movement of such data. The tests also cover the requirement for the Metering System to be able to provide a Declaration of Conformity for the Metering System if it or parts thereof are manufactured in a country outside the EU. The tests also cover the requirement for the Metering System to comply with the Law (2003:389) on electronic communication.

The following is a list of test IDs that cover the requirement "Controlling logout time":

- T-2, T-3, T-4, T-5, T-6, T-13, T-15, T-18, T-19, T-20, T-21

These tests cover the requirement by checking various aspects related to controlling logout time, such as the ability to set logout times, the ability to switch on and off switches, and the ability to detect connections that may cause overheating. Additionally, tests T-12 and T-14 require the user to measure performance during normal reporting operation and to measure consumption during different conditions to ensure the system is functioning correctly. Test T-10 requires the user to verify that the Metering Unit is deactivated upon delivery from the factory. Test T-17 requires the user to unscrew a voltage fuse on the meterboard to generate an event in the meter, and test T-19 requires the user to use a super magnet at the meter to cause an event in the meter. Test T-22 and T-23 require the user to verify that the meter's serial number is placed on a non-removable part and that the sealing device is raised and clearly visible for smooth and accessible passage of the sealing wire. Test T-25 and T-26 require the user to verify that the Metering Units are designed for wall mounting. Test T-28 requires the user to register overheating in connection, and test T-30 requires the user to verify that the Metering System is able to record and store information when certain events occur. Test T-29 and T-30 require the user to comply with various legal requirements and regulations, including those related to data protection and the EU MID Directive 2014/32/EU.
